replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the R172 SLK200 CDI OM651 generation — a single-generation engine. A typical engine replacement in the UK can cost between £3,000 and £6,100, with luxury or rare engines often exceeding this. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SLK200 CDI engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Mercedes specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The R172's compact engine bay is tight but the four-cylinder is relatively accessible.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Forced regen or cleaning — worth it

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 facelift (AdBlue equipped) with full service history
* Documented timing chain history — chain inspected or replaced
* Full service history with regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) using Mercedes-approved oil
* Injector history on pre-2015 models — Delphi injectors replaced with later units
avoidIf: * Any cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is serious and expensive
* Rough idle or limp mode — injector or EGR issues are common
* Poor service history — the OM651 is unforgiving of neglect
* AdBlue/DPF warnings without documented repair — emissions system repairs are costly

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SLK200 CDI offers the perfect blend of roadster style and diesel efficiency — 200+ bhp and 369lb ft of torque in a lightweight package. The OM651 engine is fundamentally reliable, with 200,000–300,000 km examples common. However, timing chain stretch, early injector failures, and emissions system issues are well-documented. A post-2015 facelift car with full service history and documented chain work is the buy. A neglected example with cold-start rattle is the one to walk away from.

cta.label: → Compare Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain stretch warning)
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the OM651
* Timing Chain History — Chains inspected or replaced? (critical on higher-mileage examples)
* Injector Performance — Check for rough idle or limp mode (injector/EGR warning)
* EGR System — Any hesitation or rough idle? (EGR clogging)
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* AdBlue System (Post-2015) — Any warnings? AdBlue level and age
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — water pump leaks are common
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* Vario-Roof — Check for leaks or delamination; a common SLK issue

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine (OM651) typically costs £3,500–£6,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. A timing chain replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

items.question: Is the Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine reliable?
items.answer: The OM651 is fundamentally reliable — 200,000–300,000 km examples are common — but has predictable weak points. Timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the defining issue. Early Delphi injector failures, EGR clogging, and water pump leaks are also known. A well-maintained example with documented chain and injector history is a reliable roadster.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch and guide wear (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500), early Delphi piezo injector failure (rough idle, limp mode, £300–£800 per injector), EGR clogging (hesitation, £400–£1,000), DPF and AdBlue issues on post-2015 models (£400–£1,500), and water pump/coolant leaks (£400–£800). The SLK's vario-roof can also suffer from leaks and delamination.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: SLK200 CDI values range from £6,000–£18,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £3,500–£6,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 70% Mercedes-Benz scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues (vario-roof, bodywork), assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz SLK200 CDI engine code?
items.answer: The SLK200 CDI uses the OM651 engine code — Mercedes' 2.1-litre four-cylinder turbo diesel producing 201–204 bhp. This is the same engine family used in the C-Class, E-Class and GLC diesels. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,500–£6,000). A 2014 recall addressed timing chain tensioner oil leaks in some OM651 engines. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic engine damage.
